name: Tasks
description: First-class tasks: discrete units of work assigned to an agent or human, linked to a contact/company/project. Queryable independently of the entity they touch — find all open high-priority tasks across the org with one filter call.

properties.description: Short verb-first action title. Examples: 'Send follow-up to Alice Chen', 'Classify reply from Acme Corp', 'Enrich contact: Bob at Stripe'. The memorizer uses this to identify content as a task.
Current task status. Always update to 'done', 'cancelled', or 'declined' when finishing.
Task urgency. Drives operator execution order — 'urgent' and 'high' run first.

Type of work, used by the operation runner to route the task to the right operation. Maps to operations under the matching category — e.g. 'send-email' → generate.outreach-sequence + act.* steps; 'classify-reply' → analyze.reply-sentiment.
Who executes. Conventions: 'agent' (any autonomous operator), 'human:<email>' (specific person), 'research-agent', 'rep:<owner>', 'system'. Defaults to 'agent'.
Task due date. Use memory_filter_by_property(due_date LT today) to surface overdue tasks.

Execution context — what the executing agent needs to complete this task. Constraints, references, prior context. Examples: 'Alice mentioned budget freeze in last reply — address in email', 'Use case-study CTA per March campaign data'.
Personize lookup key for the linked entity. 'email' for a contact, 'website_url' for a company, 'name' for a project/campaign/workspace, 'record_id' for any entity by internal ID.
Value of the linked entity's key. Examples: 'alice@acme.com', 'acme.com', 'Q2 Manufacturing Outreach'. Use memory_filter_by_property(custom_key_value=...) to find all tasks for an entity.
Project / campaign / workspace this task belongs to (matches the project's workspace_name). Use memory_filter_by_property(project=...) to find all tasks in a project.
Who created the task. Conventions: '<operation_name>' for tasks emitted by operations, 'human:<email>' for human-created, 'webhook:<provider>' for external triggers.
When the task transitioned to done/cancelled/declined.
Who finalized the task. Usually equal to assigned_to but may differ when reassigned.
What happened. One sentence summarizing the result. Examples: 'Email sent, reply within 4h', 'Skipped — contact opted out', 'Escalated to AE — high-intent reply'.
